summary.intent: Recreate a closed PR (#190) that was lost due to a force push, restoring the feature branch with proper history aligned to the current origin/main.
summary.outcome: Successfully rebased the feature branch onto current origin/main by identifying the repo rewrite (filter-branch/filter-repo), finding the equivalent commit mapping, applying a 16-file diff (~3865 additions) as a patch, resolving 3 E2E test conflicts, and creating a new PR with the original title and description.

repo: The main branch was rewritten (likely via git filter-branch or git filter-repo), resulting in identical commit messages but different SHAs — old and new histories share no common ancestor.
Feature branches that predate the repo rewrite will appear fully diverged from origin/main and cannot be rebased or merged conventionally without mapping old-to-new commit equivalents.
The feature touched 16 files across crates (chat, config, gateway, agents, web) and the iOS GraphQL schema, suggesting a cross-cutting feature spanning backend, frontend, and mobile.

code.finding: Had add/add merge conflicts when rebasing — feature branch version was taken as the resolution.
Had add/add merge conflicts when rebasing — feature branch version was taken as the resolution.
Had add/add merge conflicts when rebasing — feature branch version was taken as the resolution.
Required a separate explicit apply step after the main patch, suggesting it was not included in the primary feature diff range.

workflow: When a repo is rewritten (filter-repo/filter-branch), the correct recovery path is: find the equivalent new-main commit matching the old merge base, generate a git diff between that point and the feature HEAD, then apply the patch onto a fresh branch from new origin/main.
Squash-merge with --allow-unrelated-histories fails when histories are fully diverged due to add/add conflicts on every file — patch application (git apply) is more reliable in this scenario.
Creating a backup branch before any rebase/migration operation is essential when the branch state is complex or unclear.

friction: PR #190 was closed automatically when the branch was force-pushed, requiring full recreation.
Squash-merge approach (--allow-unrelated-histories) failed with add/add conflicts on every file, requiring a complete strategy change to patch-based application.
Multiple investigative steps were needed to determine the root cause (repo rewrite vs. simple divergence), involving checking roots, commit counts, and SHA comparisons across old and new histories.
The iOS schema file required a separate apply step and was not captured in the main diff range.
open_items: E2E test conflicts were resolved by taking the feature branch version wholesale — the correctness of those resolutions relative to any changes made to those files on new main was not verified.
The new PR was created without verifying CI passes on the rebased branch, which may surface issues given the history surgery performed.
The old feature branch (pre-rebase) was backed up but may need cleanup once the new PR is confirmed good.
